zh.lifeSummary: 當Король Деннамарка, Норвегии и Швеции Король Ломбардии Италии, Великий Князь Киевский Московский Всея Руси Vsevolod III "GRAND NID" Yurevich Ivanovich III於1154年10月19日出生在Российская империяМосковская губернияМосковский уезд莫斯科時，他的父親Yuri I Dolgorukiy Vladimirovich Dolgorukis61歲，母親Princesse de Byzance Hélène COMNÈNE33歲。他於大約1175年與Lyubov Vasilkovna在蘇聯РоссияВладимирская Область結婚。他於1176年被列為ruled 1176-1212。他去世於1212年4月15日在俄羅斯Владимирская ОбластьВладимир享年57歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Король Деннамарка, Норвегии и Швеции Король Ломбардии Италии, Великий Князь Киевский Московский Всея Руси Vsevolod III "GRAND NID" Yurevich Ivanovich III于1154年10月19日出生在Российская империяМосковская губернияМосковский уезд莫斯科时，他的父亲Yuri I Dolgorukiy Vladimirovich Dolgorukis61岁，母亲Princesse de Byzance Hélène COMNÈNE33岁。他于大约1175年与Lyubov Vasilkovna在苏联РоссияВладимирская Область结婚。他于1176年被列为ruled 1176-1212。他去世于1212年4月15日在俄罗斯Владимирская ОбластьВладимир享年57岁。

nameMeanings.name: Yurevich
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Russian Boris, Vladimir, Leonid, Mikhail, Lev, Dmitry, Igor, Anatoly, Arkadiy, Yefim, Arkady, Aleksandr.  Jewish Isaak, Naum, Yakov, Isak, Hyman, Sima, Zalman.
Jewish (from Belarus and Lithuania): variant of Horowitz , originating under Russian influence, since Russian has no h and alters it to g in borrowed words and names.

variants.name: Horowitz
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Gershon, Hyman, Naftali, Mayer, Baruch, Meyer, Moshe, Shlomo, Yaakov, Avram.

Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name from the town of Hořovice in central Bohemia, named with a short form of a Czech personal name such as Hořimir or Hořislav. This town was a major Jewish centre in the Middle Ages.
History: This is one of the old Ashkenazic surnames. In Eastern Europe, it was used by rabbinical families well before the mass adoption of surnames by local Jews at the turn of the 19th century.

nameMeanings.name: Ivanovich
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Ukrainian (standard transliteration Ivanovych) and Belorussian: patronymic from the personal name Ivan , Slavic form of English John .
Americanized form of Serbian and Croatian Ivanović, Slovak Ivanovič, cognates of 1 above. Compare Evanovich .

variants.name: Ivan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Romanian Vasile, Constantin, Danut, Dumitru, Floarea, Gheorghe, Viorel.

Romanian and Czech; Slovak (also Iván); Hungarian (Iván): from the personal name Ivan, Slavic form of Latin Joannes, English John . In North America, this surname may also be a shortened form of a Slavic patronymic derived from the personal name Ivan, e.g. Croatian and Serbian Ivanović (see Ivanovic ), Slovak and Slovenian Ivanič (see Ivanic ), Slovenian Ivanc (see Ivans ).

nameMeanings.name: Vsevolod
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Slavic: from vse ‘all’ + volod ‘rule’, coined as a vernacular equivalent of Greek Pankratios.

nameMeanings.name: III
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Japanese Yuko, Aiko, Hideaki, Hiroki, Hiroyuki, Isamu, Kaoru, Katsumi, Keiichiro, Kenji, Masako, Masayuki.
Japanese: variously written, sometimes with characters used phonetically; the most probable meanings are 仁井 ‘benevolent well’, 新居 ‘new residence’, or 新井 ‘new well’. It is not common in Japan.

nameMeanings.name: "GRAND
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Transferred use of the surname, common in Scotland, where it is the name of a famous clan. It is derived from a nickname meaning ‘large’ (Anglo-Norman grand). In the United States the name is sometimes bestowed in honour of the Civil War general and 18th president, Ulysses S. Grant ( 1822–85 ).

nameMeanings.name: NID"
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of Edward , originating in the misdivision of phrases such as mine Ed ( compare Nan ). It was common in the Middle Ages and up to the 18th century, but in the 19th was almost entirely superseded in the role of short form by Ted . It is now, however, enjoying a modest revival, also as an independent given name.

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

historicEvents.description: "Armies of Prince Andrey Bogolyubskiy of Vladimir-Suzdal' sack Kiev. Andrey assumes title ""Grand Prince of Kiev and all Rus'""but chooses to reside in Suzdal'."

en.lifeSummary: When Король Деннамарка, Норвегии и Швеции Король Ломбардии Италии, Великий Князь Киевский Московский Всея Руси Vsevolod III "GRAND NID" Yurevich Ivanovich III was born on 19 October 1154, in Moscow, Moscow Uyezd, Moscow, Russian Empire, his father, Yuri I Dolgorukiy Vladimirovich Dolgorukis, was 61 and his mother, Princesse de Byzance Hélène COMNÈNE, was 33. He married Lyubov Vasilkovna about 1175, in Vladimir Oblast, Russia, Soviet Union. In 1176, his occupation is listed as ruled 1176-1212. He died on 15 April 1212, in Vladimir, Vladimir Oblast, Russia, at the age of 57.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Король Деннамарка, Норвегии и Швеции Король Ломбардии Италии, Великий Князь Киевский Московский Всея Руси Vsevolod III "GRAND NID" Yurevich Ivanovich III est né le 19 octobre 1154, à Moscou, Moskovskiy Uyezd, Moscow, Empire russe, son père, Yuri I Dolgorukiy Vladimirovich Dolgorukis, avait 61 ans et sa mère, Princesse de Byzance Hélène COMNÈNE, avait 33 ans. Il a épousé Lyubov Vasilkovna environ 1175, à Vladimirskaya Oblast', Russia, Union soviétique. En 1176, sa profession de son indiquée est ruled 1176-1212. Il est décédé le 15 avril 1212, à Vladimir, Vladimirskaya Oblast', Russie, à l’âge de 57 ans.

Oktober 1154 in Moskau, Moskovskiy Uyezd, Moscow, Russisches Kaiserreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Yuri I Dolgorukiy Vladimirovich Dolgorukis 61 und seine Mutter Princesse de Byzance Hélène COMNÈNE 33. Er hat um 1175 in Vladimirskaya Oblast', Russia, Sowjetunion Lyubov Vasilkovna geheiratet. 1176 ist sein Beruf als ruled 1176-1212 aufgeführt. Er starb am 15. April 1212 im Alter von 57 Jahren in Vladimir, Vladimirskaya Oblast', Russland.

ko.lifeSummary: Король Деннамарка, Норвегии и Швеции Король Ломбардии Италии, Великий Князь Киевский Московский Всея Руси Vsevolod III "GRAND NID" Yurevich Ivanovich III(이)가 1154년 10월 19일 Российская империя Московская губерния Московский уезд Москва에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Yuri I Dolgorukiy Vladimirovich Dolgorukis(은)는 61세였고, 그의 어머니 Princesse de Byzance Hélène COMNÈNE(은)는 33세였다. 그는 대략 1175년 소련 Россия Владимирская Область에서 Lyubov Vasilkovna(와)과 결혼했다. 1176년, 그의 직업은 ruled 1176-1212이었다. 그는 1212년 4월 15일 러시아 Владимирская Область Владимир에서 57세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Король Деннамарка, Норвегии и Швеции Король Ломбардии Италии, Великий Князь Киевский Московский Всея Руси Vsevolod III "GRAND NID" Yurevich Ivanovich III nasceu em 19 de outubro de 1154, em Moskva, Moskovskiy Uyezd, Moscow, Russisches Kaiserreich, seu pai, Yuri I Dolgorukiy Vladimirovich Dolgorukis, tinha 61 anos e sua mãe, Princesse de Byzance Hélène COMNÈNE, tinha 33 anos. Ele casou-se com Lyubov Vasilkovna aproximadamente 1175, em Vladimirskaya Oblast', Russia, Sowjetunion. Em 1176, sua profissão aparece como ruled 1176-1212. Ele faleceu em 15 de abril de 1212, em Vladimir, Vladimirskaya Oblast', Rússia, com 57 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Король Деннамарка, Норвегии и Швеции Король Ломбардии Италии, Великий Князь Киевский Московский Всея Руси Vsevolod III "GRAND NID" Yurevich Ivanovich III родился 19 октября 1154 г. в местности Москва, Московский уезд, Московская губерния, Российская империя, его отцу по имени Yuri I Dolgorukiy Vladimirovich Dolgorukis было 61 год, а матери, которую звали Princesse de Byzance Hélène COMNÈNE, 33. Он женился на женщине по имени Lyubov Vasilkovna около 1175 г. в местности Владимирская Область, Россия, Советский Союз. В 1176 году он работал по профессии ruled 1176-1212. Он умер 15 апреля 1212 г. в местности Владимир, Владимирская Область, Россия в возрасте 57 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Король Деннамарка, Норвегии и Швеции Король Ломбардии Италии, Великий Князь Киевский Московский Всея Руси Vsevolod III "GRAND NID" Yurevich Ivanovich III nació el 19 de octubre de 1154, en Moscú, Moskovskiy Uyezd, Moscow, Russisches Kaiserreich, su papá, Yuri I Dolgorukiy Vladimirovich Dolgorukis, tenía 61 años y su mamá, Princesse de Byzance Hélène COMNÈNE, tenía 33 años. Se casó con Lyubov Vasilkovna aproximadamente 1175, en Vladimirskaya Oblast', Russia, Unión Soviética. En 1176, su ocupación registrada era ruled 1176-1212. Murió el 15 de abril de 1212, en Vladimir, Vladimirskaya Oblast', Rusia, a la edad de 57 años.
